2606.11290.title: FlowBank: Query-Adaptive Agentic Workflows Optimization through Precompute-and-Reuse
2606.11290.paper_summary: Large Language Model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems are increasingly powerful, but current agentic workflow optimization paradigms make an unsatisfying trade-off. Task-level methods spend substantial offline compute yet deploy only a single workflow, leaving complementary candidates unused, while query-level methods synthesize a new workflow per query at substantial inference cost. Our motivating analysis shows these paradigms are more complementary than competing: workflows discovered during offline search often solve different subsets of queries, and many queries handled by expensive query-level generation can already be solved by cheaper precomputed workflows. This suggests a different objective: rather than searching for one universally best workflow or regenerating one per instance, we should build a compact bank of reusable, complementary workflows and select among them adaptively at inference time. Doing so requires solving three coupled problems: generating complementary rather than redundant candidates, compressing them into a small deployable portfolio, and assigning each query to the right workflow under a performance-cost trade-off. To this end, we present FlowBank, a three-stage framework for portfolio-based agentic workflow optimization. Diversifying proposes DiverseFlow to steer search toward under-covered queries and produce a high-coverage candidate pool. Curating proposes CuraFlow to compress this pool into a compact portfolio with minimal redundancy. Matching casts deployment as edge-value prediction on a query-workflow bipartite graph and routes each incoming query to the portfolio member with the best predicted utility. Across five benchmarks, FlowBank achieves the highest average score among the evaluated methods while remaining cost-competitive, improving over the strongest automated and handcrafted baselines by 4.26% and 14.92% relative, respectively.
2606.11290.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）基礎的多代理系統越來越強大，但當前的代理工作流程優化範式卻做出了不令人滿意的權衡。任務級別的方法花費大量的離線計算，但僅部署單一工作流程，讓互補的候選者未被利用，而查詢級別的方法則在相當大的推理成本下為每個查詢合成新的工作流程。我們的動機分析顯示這些範式更具互補性而非競爭性：在離線搜索中發現的工作流程通常解決不同子集的查詢，而許多由昂貴的查詢級生成處理的查詢，實際上已經可以由較便宜的預計算工作流程解決。這暗示著一個不同的目標：與其尋找一個普遍最佳的工作流程或為每個實例重新生成一個，不如建立一個緊湊的可重用互補工作流程庫，並在推理時自適應地從中選擇。這樣做需要解決三個耦合問題：生成互補而非冗餘的候選者，將它們壓縮成一個小型可部署的投資組合，以及在性能與成本的權衡下將每個查詢分配給正確的工作流程。為此，我們提出了FlowBank，一個基於投資組合的代理工作流程優化的三階段框架。多樣化提出了DiverseFlow，旨在引導搜索朝向未被充分覆蓋的查詢，並產生一個高覆蓋率的候選池。策展提出了CuraFlow，將這個候選池壓縮成一個最小冗餘的緊湊投資組合。匹配將部署視為查詢-工作流程二分圖上的邊值預測，並將每個進來的查詢路由到預測效用最佳的投資組合成員。在五個基準測試中，FlowBank在評估的方法中實現了最高的平均分數，同時保持成本競爭力，分別比最強的自動化和手工基準提高了4.26%和14.92%。
2606.11290.author: Lingzhi Yuan et.al.
2606.11290.authors: Lingzhi Yuan, Chenghao Deng, Fangxu Yu, Souradip Chakraborty, Mohammad Rostami, Furong Huang
